data.title: Why Is Springview Stock (SPHL) Up Today?
data.description:
Springview (SPHL) stock rocketed higher on Thursday after the residential and commercial building designer and construction company announced a memorandum of understanding with green energy company Jiangsu GSO New Energy Technology. The two companies intend to explore a strategic partnership that would bring solar power to residential buildings in Singapore.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The potential partnership would see Springview design its residential buildings with the addition of solar panels from Jiangsu GSO New Energy Technology in mind. Jiangsu GSO New Energy Technology would also be available for engineering and support related to these solar panels.

Springview noted that it intends to launch a pilot of this collaboration with Jiangsu GSO New Energy Technology, which will allow the two companies to evaluate the potential of the partnership. However, it notes that there is no guarantee the deal will continue beyond this pilot stage.

Springview Stock Movement Today

Springview stock was up 348.44% in pre-market trading on Thursday, following a 5.46% drop yesterday. The stock has rallied 8.17% year-to-date but was down 91.99% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of SPHL stock, as more than 19 million shares changed hands, compared to a three-month daily average of about 3.85 million units.
data.author: William White

data.title: Hershey (HSY) Raises Ad Budget to Revamp Its Flagship Chocolate Bar
data.description:
Hershey (HSY) is giving its 125‑year‑old flagship chocolate bar a modern makeover, the Wall Street Journal reported. The multinational confectionery company is expected to raise its ad budget for the brand by 20% and launch its first major campaign in eight years. Hershey spent $600 million on advertising in 2024, but this year, the classic bar is getting even more attention.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For years, Hershey's relied on big holidays such as Halloween, Valentine's Day, and Easter to boost sales. The strategy worked, but it did not create much buzz. Now the company wants the brand to stay visible all year and appeal to more people, including adults without children.

Hershey Ramps Up Marketing With New Campaign

The new campaign will run across traditional TV, streaming platforms, TikTok influencer partnerships, live events, and tie‑ins with major cultural moments like the Winter Olympics and America250 celebrations. The push is expected to begin next week.

Among other marketing efforts, Hershey is stepping into Hollywood. A feature film titled Hershey, about the company's founder and his wife, is now in development.

Also, a recent limited-edition "Dubai-inspired" Hershey's bar launch in Times Square, along with a big showing at the Rockefeller Center tree‑lighting, gave an early glimpse of the brand's new, trend-focused strategy.

Hershey's big marketing push comes at a challenging period. Cocoa prices have surged since 2023, squeezing margins. Also, the company expects up to $170 million in tariff expenses this year. Moving to topline, organic net sales rose 6.2% last quarter, but Halloween sales came in weaker than expected.

Is Hershey a Buy or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, HSY stock has a Hold consensus rating based on three Buys and 13 Holds assigned in the last three months. At $198.71, the average Hershey stock price target implies a 0.21% downside risk.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: TSMC Earnings: TSM Stock Jumps after Blowout Q4 Results on AI Chip Boom
data.description:
Shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co., or TSMC, (TSM) gained over 6% in pre-market hours on Thursday after the company reported a 35% jump in Q4 profit, surpassing estimates, driven by robust demand for AI chips. For the fourth quarter ended December 31, 2025, TSMC posted NT$505.74 billion ($16 billion) in net income and diluted EPS of NT$19.50 ($3.14 per ADR). Meanwhile, revenue increased 25.5% year-over-year to NT$1.046 trillion ($33.73 billion), beating the expected NT$1.034 trillion.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

TSMC is the world's largest contract chipmaker, supplying advanced semiconductors to top tech giants, including Apple (AAPL) and Nvidia (NVDA).

TSMC Rides AI Boom

In Q4, TSMC's high-performance computing division, which covers AI and 5G applications, accounted for the majority of sales. The company said advanced chips measuring 7 nanometers or smaller represented 77% of total wafer revenue during the quarter.

For full-year 2025, these chips made up 74% of revenue, up from 69% in 2024, highlighting TSMC's ongoing shift toward cutting-edge semiconductor technology. For context, smaller nanometer sizes mean more compact transistors, which enable faster processing and greater energy efficiency.

TSMC's move toward advanced technologies is clear. Below is a screenshot showing its revenue breakdown by technology over the past few quarters.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Amazon (AMZN) Secures U.S. Copper Supply as AI Data Centers Push Demand Higher
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) is buying copper from a mine in Arizona to support its fast-growing AI data center buildout. The deal runs for two years and comes from a site run by Rio Tinto Group (RIO). Notably, this mine is the first new U.S. copper source in more than ten years. The copper will be used in wires, power gear, and other parts needed inside data centers. As a result, Amazon is taking steps to secure key inputs before supply gets tighter.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the same time, the mine uses a new process that relies on bacteria and acid to pull copper from low-grade rock. This method allows output from material that was once ignored. Rio Tinto says this approach also uses less water and creates less carbon. For Amazon, the copper volume is small, yet it adds supply certainty at a time of high demand.

Meanwhile, AMZN shares dropped 2.45% on Wednesday, closing at $236.65.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: GS Earnings: Goldman Sachs Stock Jumps on Mixed Q4 Results
data.description:
Goldman Sachs (GS) stock was up on Thursday following the release of the financial company's Q4 2025 earnings report. The company reported diluted earnings per share of $14.01, compared to Wall Street's estimate of $11.70. Investors will also note that this is a 17.2% increase from the $11.95 reported in the same period of the year prior.

Goldman Sachs also reported revenue of $13.45 billion during its most recent earnings report, compared to analysts' estimate of $14.52 billion. The company's revenue decreased 3% from the $13.87 billion reported in Q4 2024. Goldman Sachs also noted that its ROE for the quarter was up 1% year-over-year.

Goldman Sachs stock gained slightly in pre-market trading on Thursday, following a similar dip yesterday. The shares were up 6.11% year-to-date and 52.15% over the past 12 months.

data.title: Cathie Wood Invests Over $50M in Broadcom Stock (AVGO) as Wall Street Turns Upbeat
data.description:
Broadcom (AVGO) shares have been gaining momentum recently, with stock up 49% in 2005, and Cathie Wood has been steadily adding to her position. On Wednesday, January 14, her ARK Invest funds purchased 143,089 shares of Broadcom valued at about $50.74 million. The buying suggests Wood is growing more confident that demand for Broadcom's custom AI chips and networking products is picking up. Overall, Wall Street is bullish on AVGO stock and sees solid upside potential in 2026.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Top Analysts Are Upbeat About Broadcom Stock

Today, top Wells Fargo analyst Aaron Rakers upgraded the stock's rating to Overweight from Equal Weight and lifted its price target to $430, up from $410. The new price target implies about 26.5% upside from current levels. The firm said concerns about Broadcom's margins appear overdone. It also expects added support from rising demand for Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) TPUs (Tensor Processing Unit or custom accelerators), more custom AI chip orders, and steady strength in both data center networking markets.

Likewise, last week, Bernstein analyst Stacy Rasgon maintained an Outperform rating on Broadcom with a $475 price target. He said fears about new rivals and customer-built chips are overstated after a long meeting with management. Rasgon pointed to Broadcom's scale and strong supply chain in custom AI chips. He also noted that while Nvidia (NVDA) sets the pace in AI innovation, Broadcom is uniquely positioned to help customers keep up with that roadmap.

Also, Goldman Sachs analyst James Schneider added Broadcom to the firm's U.S. Conviction List. He said Broadcom's strong position in enterprise networking chips should help it gain share in custom processors used by major U.S. cloud firms. Goldman kept a Buy rating on the stock with a $450 price target, implying 35% upside from current levels.

Is AVGO Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold? 

With 28 Buys and one Hold, Broadcom stock scores a Strong Buy consensus rating. The average AVGO stock price target of $459.89 indicates 35.31% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/15/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Mixed as Tech Stocks Slip, Indexes Fall
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were trading mixed early Thursday as losses in Big Tech names dragged down major indexes. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 (SPX) were up 0.23% and 0.06%, respectively, while those on the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.09%, at 3:08 a.m. EST on January 15.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Wednesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed in the red, marking a second consecutive day of losses for them. The S&P 500 declined 0.53%, the Dow dropped 0.09%, and the Nasdaq Composite fell 1.00%.

Yesterday, Chinese authorities told domestic firms to steer clear of around 12 U.S. and Israeli software vendors on national security grounds, leading to a slump in software stocks. Microsoft (MSFT), Meta (META), and Amazon (AMZN) each dropped more than 2%. Oracle (ORCL) and Broadcom (AVGO) slid 4% each. Moreover, NVDA (NVDA) shares dropped 1.4% on news that China instructed domestic firms to avoid H200 purchases.

Banks were also among the biggest laggards. Wells Fargo (WFC) dropped 4.6%, dragging the broader market lower after reporting weaker-than-expected Q4 revenue. Citigroup (C) and Bank of America (BAC) both fell more than 3%.

On the economic front, reports on initial weekly jobless claims and retail sales will be released today.

On the earnings front, notable companies reporting today include Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), Goldman Sachs (GS), Morgan Stanley (MS), and BlackRock (BLK).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.14%. WTI crude oil futures were trending lower, hovering near $59.92 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price decreased to nearly $4,601 per ounce on Thursday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened higher today as markets focused on the ongoing meeting between U.S., Greenland, and Danish officials regarding President Donald Trump's interest in acquiring the Danish territory of Greenland.

Asia-Pacific Markets Fall

Asia-Pacific markets fell on January 15 due to the ongoing U.S.-China chip trade tensions.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 0.25%. In China, the Shanghai Composite dropped 0.33%, while the Shenzhen Component lost 0.07%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei declined 0.42%, but the Topix gained 0.68%.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: NVDA vs. AMD vs. AVGO: RBC's Top Analyst Picks the Best Chip Stock
data.description:
RBC Capital analyst Srini Pajjuri initiated coverage of the semiconductor industry, with specific focus on Nvidia (NVDA), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), and Broadcom (AVGO). He gave NVDA a "Buy" rating, while giving a "Hold" rating to AMD and AVGO shares.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Pajjuri sees "limited threat" to Nvidia's full-stack AI dominance, reinforcing his bullish stance on the stock. In contrast, he does not see many immediate catalysts for AMD until MI450 ramps for OpenAI in Q4. For Broadcom, he cited its high valuation relative to leader Nvidia as a concerning factor.

Pajjuri is a five-star analyst on TipRanks, ranking #227 out of 12,040 analysts tracked. He has a 73% success rate and an impressive average return per rating of 36.60%.

NVDA Stock: A Leader in Full-Stack AI

Pajjuri assigned a price target of $240 on NVDA, implying 31.1% upside potential from current levels. He argued that Nvidia maintains a dominant position in full-stack AI, facing limited incremental threat from recent ASIC advancements and competitors such as AMD. He does not anticipate any sharp, widespread slowdown in hyperscaler capital expenditure, but argues that any deceleration would be gradual and already largely reflected in current valuations.

Additionally, he has higher conviction in Nvidia's order book relative to its peer group, supporting the thesis that Nvidia's revenue outlook remains robust despite evolving competitive dynamics.

AMD Stock: No Immediate Catalysts

Pajjuri assigned a $230 price target to AMD, signaling only a modest 2.9% upside potential from current levels. He views the OpenAI partnership as a pivotal validation of AMD's viability as a GPU supplier for hyperscale initiatives, signaling credible competitive positioning against incumbents like Nvidia.

However, despite management's strong confidence in achieving an initial 1GW production ramp, Pajjuri believes that the stock price already factors in that growth, limiting near-term upside potential. His analysis highlights a scarcity of immediate catalysts, with meaningful momentum expected only after the MI450 chips ramp up for OpenAI in Q4. Overall, he has a cautious view due to risks in pulling it off.

AVGO Stock: High Valuation Relative to Peers

Pajjuri set a $370 price target on AVGO, implying 8.9% upside potential. He acknowledged robust near-term momentum in Broadcom's TPU (Tensor Processing Unit or custom accelerators) segment, driven by hyperscaler adoption. However, Pajjuri flags uncertainties surrounding the durability of the Anthropic production ramp, alongside opaque timing and scale for the prospective OpenAI engagement, tempering long-term growth visibility.

Moreover, he highlighted that Broadcom's valuation trades at a 25% premium to Nvidia even following recent market corrections, suggesting limited relative appeal amid comparable AI exposure.

NVDA, AMD, AVGO: Which Is the Best Chip Stock?

The TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool shows that analysts have assigned a "Strong Buy" consensus rating on three stocks, with NVDA offering the highest upside potential over the next twelve months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth
